items.title: Wildfire containment operations continue in Latakia countryside in Syria
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Syrian Civil Defense units, supported by teams from neighboring countries, remain deployed across the affected areas, to contain the blazes and limit environmental damage. The fires, which started in early July, have proven difficult to bring under control despite reinforcements from Jordan, Turkey and Lebanon. 

(AP video by Ghaith Elsayyed; Production by Ali Sharafeddine)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Explosion and smoke seen over the Gaza skyline
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Israeli military said on Saturday that over the past 48 hours, troops struck approximately 250 targets in the Gaza Strip, including militants, booby-trapped structures, weapons storage facilities, anti-tank missile launch posts, sniper posts, tunnels and additional Hamas sites. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Russia drone and cruise missile attacks kill at least 2 in Ukraine
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Ukrainian air force on Saturday reported intercepting 319 drones and 25 cruise missiles, while hundreds of decoys were jammed. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: 'Love Island USA' season 6 winner Serena Page recalls learning her season was a hit
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>"Love Island USA" season 6 winner Serena Page says she was shocked to learn her season had more viewers than shows like “Bridgerton” and “House of the Dragon.” Now, some of that cast are starring in a spinoff called "Love Island: Beyond the Villa."
#loveisland #loveisaldusa #serenapage #shorts</article>

items.title: It's not Jurassic Park. So what does 'de-extinction' mean? #science
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>“Lord of the Rings” filmmaker Peter Jackson is backing a project to resurrect New Zealand's extinct giant moa. But if it doesn't look like Jurassic Park, then what does "de-extinction" actually mean?</article>

items.title: Relatives mourn for those killed in Israeli airstrikes on central Gaza Strip
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thirteen people were killed in Deir al-Balah, in central Gaza, after Israeli airstrikes pounded the area starting late Friday, officials in al-Aqsa Martyr's Hospital said. 

(AP video by Abdel Kareem Hana; Production by Wafaa Shurafa)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Empty osprey nests and starving chicks have some blaming human overfishing
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The osprey in and around Chesapeake Bay made a stunning comeback after now-banned pesticides almost wiped them out. Now, dying chicks and empty nests have some wondering if human overfishing is driving them back to the brink. (AP Video/Allen G. Breed)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: North Korean leader meets with Russia’s foreign minister in Wonsan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>North Korean leader Kim Jong Un on Saturday received Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov in Wonsan. Earlier, Lavrov held talks with his North Korean counterpart as part of the second round of the bilateral foreign ministerial strategic dialogue.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Turkey’s leader says “new page in history” has opened as Kurdish militant group starts disarmament
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Kurdistan Workers’ Party, or PKK, held a symbolic ceremony on Friday in northern Iraq. It was the first concrete step toward a promised disarmament, as part of a peace process.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: People run with charging bulls at Pamplona's San Fermín festival
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Five runners have been taken to hospital after the sixth bull run at the San Fermin festival in Pamplona, Spain. One participant was knocked unconscious by a bull inside the ring at the end of the event. (AP video by Miguel Oses)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Russia’s Lavrov visits North Korea, holds talks with counterpart
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Sergey Lavrov began his three-day trip to North Korea on Friday. Russia's Tass news agency reported Wednesday that the trip was part of the second round of “strategic dialogue” with North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Colombia authorities arrest alleged leader of Italian ’ndrangheta mafia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Colombian authorities said Friday they captured an alleged leader of the Italian ’ndrangheta mafia in Latin America who is accused of overseeing cocaine shipments and managing illegal trafficking routes to Europe.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: Recaptured leader of Ecuador's Los Choneros criminal gang agrees to be extradited to US
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Adolfo Macías Villamar appeared via videolink before the President of the National Court of Justice Jose Suing who headed the hearing in Quito after receiving a formal extradition request from the U.S.

items.title: LIVE: Funeral procession for Idaho firefighters killed while responding to wildfire
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Live funeral procession for the Idaho firefighters who were killed after they were shot while responding to a wildfire near Coeur d'Alene, Idaho. Credit: KXLY</article>
